[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2016
[TITLE]: Sex offender selling Nazi flag is booted from county fair
[METADATA/INTRO]: Sex offender selling Nazi flag is booted from county fair BLOOMSBURG, Pa. — A Pennsylvania fair vendor, who turned out to be a registered sex offender, was booted from the event after visitors complained that he was selling Nazi flags....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Patrons later posted photos of the booth showing that the Nazi flag had been replaced with a Confederate flag. 
 For Luzerne Co., Trump is a gamble worth taking 
 By Monday afternoon, officials decided to shut down the booth entirely after deeming other items, including bumper stickers, offensive. 
 "We like this to be a clean, American fair," Reichart said. "We're family-oriented and we don't tolerate these things." 
 The Associated Press was unable to locate a phone number for Betsinger, but he told Philadelphia magazine he has sold such items for 45 years and he doesn't see why it is a problem just because some people complained.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2016
[TITLE]: Roby Trump Mobile a Big Country original
[METADATA/INTRO]: Roby Trump Mobile a Big Country original Bob Terry shares history with that Fisher County intersection...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: But this Mad Max-looking yellow vehicle in the middle dwarfs all, festooned as it is in flags and homemade signs. On the back across a piece of red plywood, in white (mostly) capital letters, was painted “Trump Mobile.” Above it, another read “Jesus Saves." Floating in the breeze, a large Trump flag hung from a brass pipe. 
 “Who owns the Trump Mobile?” I asked, after crossing the street to the convenience store. 
 “Bob Terry,” a woman replied. 
 “He’s the crankiest, most cantankerous old man you’ll ever meet.” 
 I liked him already. Soon I had his phone number, because it’s Roby. I met him a week later and found him affable and impassioned, if a bit salty.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2017-08-28
[TITLE]: Bloomsburg Fair bans Nazi flags – Wilkes-Barre Citizens' Voice
[METADATA/INTRO]: BLOOMSBURG – When the 162nd Bloomsburg Fair opens next month, Nazi flags will not be among the items on display to the public, according to organizers. Whether Confederate merchandise will be available, however, remains to be seen. Last September, the...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: BLOOMSBURG – When the 162nd Bloomsburg Fair opens next month, Nazi flags will not be among the items on display to the public, according to organizers. Whether Confederate merchandise will be available, however, remains to be seen. 
 Last September, the fair found itself embroiled in controversy after a vendor who turned out to be a registered sex offender displayed a swastika flag alongside a banner supporting Donald Trump’s presidential bid. Confronted with a social media firestorm, organizers ejected the vendor, whose wares included what he described as “German history flags” as well as Confederate battle flags and other controversial materials.
